# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import express = require('express'); // The following line must be imported first! It must come first.
import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

function middleware(req: any, res:any, next) {
  // req.header('Authorization') == "Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
  const tokenString = req.headers['authorization'];

  let decoded;

  try {
    decoded = jwt.decode(tokenString);

    console.log(decoded); // the payload part of the JWT should be decoded (without secrets!)

    res.json({
      success: true,
      userinfo: 'user id',
      token: req.headers['authorization']
    });
  } catch (err) {
    next();
  }
}


export const middleware = middleware;
